title: tarenna pembensis
intro: Tarenna pembensis, also known as Pembroke's tarenna, is a flowering plant species in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Sri Lanka and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Tarenna pembensis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. In herbal preparations, Tarenna pembensis is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems can be dried and used in teas or capsules.
